role: Former Iranian Ambassador to Australia; expelled September 2025
bio: Ahmad Sadeghi served as Iran's Ambassador to Australia. In September 2025, the Australian government expelled him — the first ambassador-level expulsion by Australia since World War II — after the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) assessed that the IRGC had directed two antisemitic arson attacks in Sydney and Melbourne. The expulsion marked a significant escalation in Australia's response to Iranian intelligence activities on Australian soil and drew widespread international attention as a historically unprecedented diplomatic action from Canberra.

sources.title: Australia expels Iranian ambassador over IRGC-linked arson attacks
Australia expels Iranian ambassador after ASIO links IRGC to arson attacks

editorial_notes: Exact expulsion date September 2025. First ambassador-level expulsion from Australia since WWII. ASIO assessment linked IRGC to Sydney and Melbourne arson attacks.
